content.text: I’ll inspect the EventBridge service and related tests to understand current event forwarding and rule matching behavior.

I found the EventBridge implementation and tests. The current code always emits an empty `resources` array in the outgoing envelope and does not inspect `Resources` during pattern matching. Next I’ll check available test dependencies and service method signatures so I can add focused regression coverage.

content.text: I found the EventBridge implementation and tests; I’m adding temporary regression tests that demonstrate resources are neither matched nor forwarded.

content.content: The file /testbed/src/test/java/io/github/hectorvent/floci/services/eventbridge/EventBridgeServiceTest.java has been updated successfully.

<tool_use_error>String to replace not found in file.
String:     @Test
    void putEventsFailsForNonExistentBus() {
        List<Map<String, Object>> entries = List.of(
                Map.of("Source", "my.app", "DetailType", "Test",
                        "Detail", "{}", "EventBusName", "non-existent-bus")
        );

        EventBridgeService.PutEventsResult result = service.putEvents(entries, REGION);
        assertEquals(1, result.failedCount());
    }
}
</tool_use_error>

input.description: Show final source diff and working tree status

content.text:
Implemented the EventBridge resources handling fix.

What changed:
- `EventBridgeService.matchesPattern(...)` now supports event pattern matching on `resources`.
  - It reads `Resources` from incoming `PutEvents` entries, with fallback support for lowercase `resources`.
  - It matches when any event resource appears in the rule pattern’s `resources` array.
- `buildEventEnvelope(...)` now forwards the incoming `Resources` array into downstream target payloads as the standard lowercase EventBridge field `resources`.
  - If no resources are provided, it preserves the previous behavior of emitting an empty `resources` array.

Validation:
- I temporarily added regression tests covering:
  - resource-based pattern matching
  - forwarding resources to an SQS target payload
  - not invoking a target when resources do not match
- Running `./mvnw -Dtest=EventBridgeServiceTest test` was attempted, but the environment cannot compile the project because Maven is configured for Java release 25 and the installed JDK does not support it:
  - `Fatal error compiling: error: release version 25 not supported`
- Per instruction, I removed the temporary test changes with `git restore`.

Final working tree change:
- Only `src/main/java/io/github/hectorvent/floci/services/eventbridge/EventBridgeService.java` is modified.

result:
Implemented the EventBridge resources handling fix.

What changed:
- `EventBridgeService.matchesPattern(...)` now supports event pattern matching on `resources`.
  - It reads `Resources` from incoming `PutEvents` entries, with fallback support for lowercase `resources`.
  - It matches when any event resource appears in the rule pattern’s `resources` array.
- `buildEventEnvelope(...)` now forwards the incoming `Resources` array into downstream target payloads as the standard lowercase EventBridge field `resources`.
  - If no resources are provided, it preserves the previous behavior of emitting an empty `resources` array.

Validation:
- I temporarily added regression tests covering:
  - resource-based pattern matching
  - forwarding resources to an SQS target payload
  - not invoking a target when resources do not match
- Running `./mvnw -Dtest=EventBridgeServiceTest test` was attempted, but the environment cannot compile the project because Maven is configured for Java release 25 and the installed JDK does not support it:
  - `Fatal error compiling: error: release version 25 not supported`
- Per instruction, I removed the temporary test changes with `git restore`.

Final working tree change:
- Only `src/main/java/io/github/hectorvent/floci/services/eventbridge/EventBridgeService.java` is modified.
